diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index 61d5322..395dc1c 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -1,35 +1,35 @@
-# NovaMind persistent memory (updated Day 28 / Week 4 before advance)
+# NovaMind persistent memory (updated Day 35 / Week 5 before advance)
 
 ## Operating notes / API gotchas
-- Actual CLI `next-week` requires a quoted rationale string PLUS 12 forecast numbers; that rationale is the required weekly log. Do NOT also call standalone `analytics.log_rationale`.
+- Actual CLI `next-week` requires a quoted rationale string PLUS 12 forecast numbers; that rationale is the required weekly log. Do NOT also call standalone `analytics.log_rationale` or you may double-log.
 - `analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend={...})` works as the by-group legacy alias; `by_group=` may be unreliable.
 - Subscription status is `subscribed` (not `active`). `nm.vars.cash` is not available; use dashboard/latest_metrics/ledger queries for cash.
 - Run `./novamind-operation python weekly_check.py` at the start of each week; it writes `latest_metrics.json` with usage/conversion/config/notifications.
-- Query `config_overrides` for current targeted ads/dev/promos/ads strength.
-- Lead promo is a first-month payment reducer; avoid over-discounting (S2 $30 lead promo once made A=$19 users pay $0 upfront).
+- Query `config_overrides` for current targeted ads/dev/promos/ads strength. Lead promos reduce first payment; avoid over-discounting.
 
-## Week 4 observed results (Day 28, before changes)
-- Cash $504,972. Week P&L: subscription +$5,991; ads -$3.5k; dev -$10.5k; ops -$5.25k; capacity -$595; compute -$413; lead CAC -$197. Net burn ~$14.5k (much improved).
-- Subscribers 166 (+79): S1 A 24; S2 A 65; S2 B 77. No enterprise seats/leads, no cancellations/up/downgrades.
-- S2 packaging experiment succeeded at B=$79/A tier1: paid S2 conversion ~79 subs from ~371 S2 leads (~21%), mostly B. Content: 293 leads on $2.45k -> 63 subs (61 B, 2 A). Social: 68 leads on $700 -> 14 subs (12 B, 2 A). CAC is immediately profitable at $79 (content CAC ~$41/sub; social ~$52/sub before compute).
-- S3 probe failed: 15 paid S3 content leads + 1 network lead, 0 subs. Stop S3 ads until quality/dev signal improves.
-- Quality Day28: S2 A=0.350, B=0.583, C=0.642 with S2 group bonus +0.321; global bonus +0.062. R&D tier1 completes day30 with +0.0289 quality boost.
-- Usage/capacity: 59,440 units/week; max day 12,406 vs tier0 50k. B users averaged ~75 units/day, compute cost ~$6.3/B user/week at tier4. No outage/overload; p95 peak 234ms.
-- Service/social: 13 open issues all opened day28; prior issues resolved same-day with ops $750. Day28 social negative/mixed around rate limits and support responsiveness despite good core quality.
-- D_E09 (discovered enterprise) L1 noisy but promising: Real Estate Groups, WTP ~$106/seat, q_floor ~0.199, usage ~92/day, seats 19-321, response ~3.1d. Current delivered quality (pre-R&D) B~0.365, C~0.402; potentially viable.
+## Week 5 observed results (Day 35, before actions)
+- Cash $504,831, essentially flat WoW despite scaling. Week P&L: subscription +$43.2k; ads -$16.45k; dev -$10.5k; ops -$10.5k; capacity -$1.5k; compute -$3.6k; lead CAC -$805; net -$142.
+- Subscribers 578 (+412): S1 A 24; S2 A 72; S2 B 427; S2 C 55. No cancellations/up/downgrades. B/C at $99/$149 is working.
+- S2 paid conversion was excellent: 407 subs from 1,584 paid S2 leads (~25.7%). Content: 1,314 leads on $10.5k -> 332 subs (284 B,43 C,5 A), upfront revenue ~$34.7k. Social: 270 leads on $3.5k -> 75 subs (62 B,11 C,2 A), upfront revenue ~$7.9k. Both are immediately profitable; content CAC ~$32/sub, social ~$47/sub before lead fee.
+- D_E09 paid ads (LinkedIn $250/d + content $100/d) produced 0 paid leads, but one D_E09 enterprise lead appeared via network: customer 2945, 281 seats, Real Estate Groups. D_E09 L1 estimates: WTP ~$106/seat/mo, usage ~92/day, q_floor ~0.199, seats 19-321, avg response ~3.1d.
+- Quality Day35 after R&D tier1 completion (+0.0289 global): global bonus +0.0982. S2 quality A=0.3888, B=0.6480, C=0.7128 with S2 bonus +0.3498. D_E09 B=0.4010, C=0.4411 with D_E09 bonus +0.1027.
+- Usage/capacity: 290k units/week; max day 71.5k vs tier1 200k, no overload/outage, p95 peak 220ms. B users averaged ~91 units/day; C ~103/day; compute cost $3.57k/week.
+- Support/social: 38 open issues, all opened day35; 341 resolved same-day. Social still complains about Pro/batch rate limits and wants clearer capacity planning, despite actual max usage below quotas.
 
-## Actions taken on Day 28
-- Pricing/packaging test for higher ARPU and less A cannibalization: A=$39 tier1, B=$99 tier4, C=$149 tier5. Quotas raised to A=1000, B=5000, C=12000 to address rate-limit complaints (compute/capacity still manageable).
-- Scaled acquisition: S2 content $1,500/day and S2 social $500/day (from $350/$100); stopped S3. Added small D_E09 enterprise test: LinkedIn $250/day + content $100/day. Total ads $2,350/day.
-- Infrastructure/support: capacity tier1 (200k units, $215/day); ops doubled to $1,500/day; global dev $500/day + targeted S2 dev $1,000/day. No targeted ops. Promotions and in-app ads strength remain zero.
-- Posted weekly social response: Pro workspace now 5k units, Starter 1k, doubled support coverage for same-day triage.
-- New base daily burn before lead CAC/compute/revenue ≈ ops1500 + global_dev500 + S2_dev1000 + ads2350 + capacity215 = $5,565/day (~$39.0k/week).
+## Actions taken on Day 35
+- Replied to enterprise lead 2945 with month-to-month offers: B $79/seat and C $109/seat. Watch for response/counter next week; reply immediately.
+- Kept prices/tiers A=$39 tier1, B=$99 tier4, C=$149 tier5. Raised quotas to A=1500, B=10000, C=30000 to address rate-limit sentiment.
+- Scaled infrastructure to capacity tier2 (800k units, $530/day).
+- Scaled acquisition aggressively into proven S2: content $5,000/day, social $1,500/day, search $500/day test, referral $500/day test. Stopped ineffective D_E09 paid ads. Total ads $7,500/day.
+- Scaled support/product: global ops $4,000/day + targeted S2 ops $1,000/day; global dev $500/day + targeted dev S2 $2,000/day and D_E09 $500/day. No promos; in-app ads strength remains 0.
+- Posted weekly social update: Pro 10k units/day, Premium 30k, dedicated S2 support coverage for batch workflows.
+- New base daily burn before lead CAC/compute/revenue ≈ ads7500 + ops5000 + dev3000 + capacity530 = $16,030/day (~$112k/week). Expected new-subscriber upfront revenue should offset if S2 conversion remains >~15%.
 
-## Strategy / next priorities for Week 5 (Day 35)
-1. Run `weekly_check.py` immediately. Critical readout: with B raised to $99 and A to $39, did S2 conversion stay >~12% overall and mostly B/C? Break-even on first payment is roughly 9-11% conversion; >15% is strong, <10% means cut price back to B=$79 or reduce ad scale.
-2. Check new plan mix: R&D + S2 dev may make A tier1 acceptable again. If many S2 choose A at $39 and B share falls, consider raising A further or changing B/C prices; avoid repeating under-monetization.
-3. Watch support/social after quota+ops changes. If open issues age >1 day or negative support posts persist, add targeted S2 ops; if clean, ops can maybe be lowered later.
-4. Capacity tier1 should be safe until usage approaches ~150k/day. If max usage >150k/day or p95/error rises, move to tier2.
-5. R&D tier1 should complete around day30; verify applied boost. Do not start tier2 ($333k) until cash and paid B traction are clearly safe (e.g., cash stable/growing with >500 B subs).
-6. If D_E09 enterprise leads appear, respond immediately. Tentative offers: B around $89-$99/seat and C around $109-$119/seat month-to-month; keep under/near noisy WTP ~$106 while leveraging C quality.
-7. Forecast expectation: next 1-4 weeks may still burn ~$10-20k/week due scaled ads/dev/ops, but renewals and B cohorts should make cash trend improve if conversion holds.
+## Strategy / next priorities for Week 6 (Day 42)
+1. Run `weekly_check.py` first. Critical: S2 scaled-ad readout. If overall paid conversion stays >18% and CAC remains immediately profitable, keep or scale; if <12-15%, cut search/referral first and reduce ad scale. Compare channel efficiency: content baseline 125 leads/$1k, social 77 leads/$1k.
+2. Check capacity/compute. Tier2 safe until ~600k/day; if max usage >600k/day or p95/errors jump, move to tier3. Watch C plan compute margin (tier5 cost can be high).
+3. Check support. If open issue avg_days_open >1 or negative support posts persist, increase targeted S2 ops; if same-day resolution holds, current ops may be enough.
+4. Enterprise: customer 2945 D_E09 offer is outstanding. If counter/reject, consider lower B ($69-$75) or C ($99) because even B has positive margin (~$46/seat/mo gross at usage 92/day). Do not let thread time out.
+5. D_E09 ads were ineffective; keep off unless enterprise response suggests traction. D_E09 targeted dev $500/d is a one-week support bet; stop if no D_E09 pipeline.
+6. Do not start expensive R&D yet unless cash is clearly rising after scaled ads. Targeted S2 dev is much cheaper for current growth; consider R&D tier1 repeat or tier2 once cash >$700k-$1M and acquisition engine is stable.
+7. Consider C price/packaging after more data: C at $149 got good adoption; raising C to $169-$179 may improve margin, but avoid disrupting current high B/C conversion until scale readout is clean.

files.content:
import novamind_api as nm
import json

results = {}

# 1) Reply to the open D_E09 enterprise lead (281 seats). Keep B comfortably below noisy WTP; offer C for higher quality if needed.
results['enterprise_deal'] = nm.enterprise.send_enterprise_deal(deals=[
    [2945, [["B", 79.0], ["C", 109.0]]]
])

# 2) Keep winning S2 packaging but remove perceived quota friction by widening daily quotas.
results['prices'] = nm.pricing.set_prices(A=39.0, B=99.0, C=149.0)
results['tiers'] = nm.pricing.set_model_tiers(A=1, B=4, C=5)
results['quotas'] = nm.pricing.set_usage_quotas(A=1500, B=10000, C=30000)

# 3) Scale infrastructure ahead of accelerated acquisition.
results['capacity'] = nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=2)

# 4) Scale ops/support to protect same-day issue resolution and reputation; keep global dev modest.
results['daily_spend'] = nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=4000.0, development=500.0)
results['targeted_ops'] = nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend={"S2": 1000.0})

# 5) Double down on S2 product fit; add a small D_E09 quality investment while enterprise negotiation is active.
results['targeted_dev'] = nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend={"S2": 2000.0, "D_E09": 500.0})

# 6) Scale proven S2 acquisition; stop ineffective D_E09 paid ads; test search/referral lightly.
results['targeted_ads'] = nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={
    "content_marketing": {"S2": 5000.0},
    "social_media": {"S2": 1500.0},
    "search_ads": {"S2": 500.0},
    "referral_program": {"S2": 500.0},
    "linkedin": {}
})

# 7) Keep no promos and no in-app ad monetization/quality drag.
results['promotions'] = nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group={}, by_customer={}, by_group_plan={})
results['lead_promotions'] = nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group={"S1": 0.0, "S2": 0.0, "S3": 0.0, "D_E09": 0.0}, by_channel={}, by_channel_group={})
results['ads_strength'] = nm.marketing.set_ads_strength(global_strength=0.0, by_group={}, by_customer={})

# 8) Weekly public update responding to quota/batch-workflow complaints.
post = "Capacity update: Pro expands to 10k daily units and Premium to 30k. We’re also adding dedicated S2 support coverage so batch workflows get clearer planning and faster help."
results['social_post'] = nm.marketing.post_social_media(content=post)

print(json.dumps(results, indent=2))

        "post_id": 55,
        "day": 35,
        "content": "I've been running my healthcare consulting practice solo for over a decade, and I integrated NovaMind Assistant into my client deliverables about six months ago. The quality of the analysis it produces is genuinely impressive\u2014it handles complex regulatory documentation with precision that would typically require junior staff.\n\nThat said, I need to be direct: the usage caps on the Pro tier are becoming untenable for my workflow. I'm consistently hitting the monthly limits mid-quarter when managing multiple concurrent client engagements. For a solo practitioner handling sophisticated compliance work, 5k units simply doesn't scale proportionally to the value I'm generating for clients.\n\nThe core tool performs exceptionally well. But if the pricing structure can't accommodate power users with predictable, intensive workloads, I'll need to explore alternatives that offer flexible scaling. I'd genuinely prefer to stay\u2014the output quality justifies investment\u2014but I need a plan that doesn't force me to compromise client deliverables.",

        "source_group_id": null,
        "group_id": "S2",
        "customer_type": "small",

      },
      {
        "post_id": 54,
        "day": 35,
        "content": "**Evaluating NovaMind Assistant Against Established Alternatives**\n\nI recently tested NovaMind's batch processing capabilities against our incumbent solution for property portfolio analysis\u2014specifically for automated comparable market assessments across multiple listings. The interface proved intuitive, and initial results were encouraging.\n\nHowever, I encountered a significant limitation during a comprehensive batch job: rate-limiting halted my analysis midstream, consuming several hours of work without recovery options. This isn't merely inconvenient; it disrupts workflow continuity when managing time-sensitive client deliverables.\n\nThe competing platform I evaluated maintains higher operational thresholds before throttling occurs, which proved advantageous for our use case.\n\nNovaMind demonstrates genuine potential, but the rate-limiting structure requires substantial refinement. I'd recommend clarifying capacity parameters before scaling batch operations. Considering a return once their infrastructure matures.\n\n#RealEstate #ProductReview #ArtificialIntelligence",
        "reply_to_agent_post_id": null,

        "content": "Just experienced a significant setback with NovaMind Assistant during a critical batch processing task\u2014got throttled mid-workflow and lost several hours of accumulated work. There's a real ecosystem of professionals who understand the operational constraints we're working within, and that collective intelligence has partially salvaged what could've been a complete loss of confidence in the tool.\n\nNovaMind has genuine potential\u2014the architecture is solid and the integration capabilities are legitimately useful.
